SEARCH
Package com.example.eventsapp

Class NotificationLogItem

java.lang.Object
com.example.eventsapp.NotificationLogItem
public class NotificationLogItem extends Object
POJO representing a document in the global notification_logs Firestore collection. Each document groups all recipients of the same notification type for the same event.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    NotificationLogItem()
    No-argument constructor required for Firestore deserialization.
    NotificationLogItem(String organizerName, String organizerId, String title, String message, String eventId, String eventName, String type, com.google.firebase.Timestamp timestamp, List<Map<String,String>> recipients)
    Constructs a fully populated NotificationLogItem.

  • Constructor Details

    • NotificationLogItem

      public NotificationLogItem()
      No-argument constructor required for Firestore deserialization.

    • NotificationLogItem

      public NotificationLogItem(String organizerName, String organizerId, String title, String message, String eventId, String eventName, String type, com.google.firebase.Timestamp timestamp, List<Map<String,String>> recipients)
      Constructs a fully populated NotificationLogItem.
      Parameters:
      organizerName - the display name of the organizer who sent the notification
      organizerId - the Firestore document ID of the organizer
      title - the notification title shown to recipients
      message - the notification body shown to recipients
      eventId - the Firestore document ID of the related event
      eventName - the human-readable name of the related event
      type - the notification category (e.g., "INVITATION", "UPDATE")
      timestamp - the Firestore server timestamp when the notification was sent
      recipients - a list of recipient maps; each map contains at minimum a "name" and "userId" entry

  • Method Details

    • getRecipientNames

      public List<String> getRecipientNames()
      Extracts and returns just the display names from the recipients list. Entries with a null or empty "name" value are silently skipped.
      Returns:
      a mutable list of recipient display names; never null

    • getOrganizerName

      public String getOrganizerName()
      Returns:
      the display name of the organizer who sent this notification

    • setOrganizerName

      public void setOrganizerName(String organizerName)
      Parameters:
      organizerName - the display name of the organizer

    • getOrganizerId

      public String getOrganizerId()
      Returns:
      the Firestore document ID of the organizer

    • setOrganizerId

      public void setOrganizerId(String organizerId)
      Parameters:
      organizerId - the Firestore document ID of the organizer

    • getTitle

      public String getTitle()
      Returns:
      the notification title shown to recipients

    • setTitle

      public void setTitle(String title)
      Parameters:
      title - the notification title

    • getMessage

      public String getMessage()
      Returns:
      the notification body shown to recipients

    • setMessage

      public void setMessage(String message)
      Parameters:
      message - the notification body

    • getEventId

      public String getEventId()
      Returns:
      the Firestore document ID of the related event

      public void setEventId(String eventId)
      Parameters:
      eventId - the Firestore document ID of the related event

    • getEventName

      public String getEventName()
      Returns:
      the human-readable name of the related event

    • setEventName

      public void setEventName(String eventName)
      Parameters:
      eventName - the human-readable event name

    • getType

      public String getType()
      Returns:
      the notification category (e.g., "INVITATION", "UPDATE")

    • setType

      public void setType(String type)
      Parameters:
      type - the notification category

    • getTimestamp

      public com.google.firebase.Timestamp getTimestamp()
      Returns:
      the Firestore server timestamp recording when the notification was dispatched

    • setTimestamp

      public void setTimestamp(com.google.firebase.Timestamp timestamp)
      Parameters:
      timestamp - the Firestore server timestamp of dispatch

    • getRecipients

      public List<Map<String,String>> getRecipients()
      Returns the raw recipients list where each element is a map containing at minimum "name" and "userId" entries.
      Returns:
      the recipients list, or null if not yet set

    • setRecipients

      public void setRecipients(List<Map<String,String>> recipients)
      Replaces the recipients list.
      Parameters:
      recipients - a list of recipient maps; each map should contain at minimum "name" and "userId" entries
